criteria.label: Drop into pdb with `breakpoint()` and navigate using n / s / r / c / p / w / u / d.
criteria.example: `breakpoint()` before the suspicious call; at the prompt, `pp messages` then `c`.

criteria.label: Use `python -m pdb -c continue script.py` (or `pytest --pdb`) for post-mortem debugging.
criteria.example: Crashing pipeline: `python -m pdb -c continue pipeline.py` → land at the failing frame.

criteria.label: Set conditional breakpoints to handle hot-loop debugging.
criteria.example: `(Pdb) b chunker.py:42, doc.id == 'doc-472'` stops only at the relevant iteration.

criteria.label: Swap `breakpoint()` to `ipdb` via `PYTHONBREAKPOINT=ipdb.set_trace` for tab completion.
Add lint rules (ruff T100) so committed `breakpoint()` calls fail CI.
criteria.example: `select = ['T100']` in `pyproject.toml`; pre-commit blocks the offending file.

criteria.label: Recognise when pdb is the wrong tool (async, multi-thread, production) and reach for logging or an IDE debugger instead.
criteria.example: Async pipeline: use VS Code's task-aware debugger or `aiomonitor`, not plain pdb.
